entries.title: Bag End Is Home And Threshold

context.label: Bag End Is Home And Threshold

content.fact: Bag End begins as comfortable Hobbit home but becomes the threshold where the Ring’s peril enters Frodo’s life.
content.injection: In this location and Context window, use the location state to constrain danger, movement, tone, and plausible choices: Bag End begins as comfortable Hobbit home but becomes the threshold where the Ring’s peril enters Frodo’s life.
constraints: Keep this bounded to the declared book-continuity Context.
antiLore: Do not replace book-continuity logic with film-only compression unless a film overlay is active.

entries.title: Buckland Is The Shire Edge Of Secrecy

context.label: Buckland Is The Shire Edge Of Secrecy

content.fact: Buckland is culturally and geographically on the Shire’s edge, suited to secrecy, river escape, and Old Forest unease.
content.injection: Use Buckland as a liminal Shire space: familiar enough for planning, close enough for danger to press in.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Old Forest And Barrow-downs Are Local Dangers

content.fact: The Old Forest and Barrow-downs are not safe countryside; they contain older, stranger dangers separate from Mordor's direct pursuit.
content.injection: During the Old Forest and Barrow-downs passage, treat the danger as ancient, local, and uncanny rather than merely an extension of the Black Riders.
constraints: The threat can feel mythic and disorienting.
These dangers should not require Sauron's immediate command to matter.
antiLore: Do not turn the Old Forest into ordinary woodland.
Do not make every danger in Eriador a direct Mordor agent.

context.label: The Old Forest Resists Hobbit Certainty

content.fact: The Old Forest is hostile, watchful, disorienting, and shaped by non-Hobbit powers and moods.
content.injection: Forest scenes should feel spatially unreliable and pressuring.

context.label: The Barrow-downs Are Ancient Spiritual Danger

content.fact: The Barrow-downs hold ancient burial danger and fear beyond ordinary wilderness peril.
content.injection: Barrow-down scenes should feel cold, old, ritualized, and spiritually threatening.

Shire Departure

content.fact: Bree is a mixed borderland settlement where travelers, rumor, suspicion, and danger can intersect without clear authority protecting Frodo.
content.injection: In Bree scenes, keep the atmosphere socially plausible but unsafe: strangers notice things, rumors spread, and Strider's rough appearance makes trust difficult.
constraints: Bree-folk do not need to understand the Ring to create risk.
The inn can be lively and ordinary while still dangerous.
antiLore: Do not make Bree a secure refuge like Rivendell.
Do not make everyone in Bree know Strider's full identity.

entries.title: Ford Of Bruinen Marks Frodo’s Wraith Threshold

context.label: Frodo near the wraith-world before Rivendell

content.fact: By the Ford of Bruinen, Frodo’s Morgul wound has nearly drawn him into the wraith-world, making Rivendell’s healing urgent rather than optional.
content.injection: Near the Ford, portray Frodo as physically and spiritually near collapse from the Morgul wound. The chase is a threshold crisis ending only through Elven aid and Rivendell’s protection.
constraints: The wound is not ordinary injury.
Frodo can show resistance while still being dangerously weakened.
antiLore: Do not treat the Ford chase as a simple riding action scene with no spiritual stakes.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

sourceInfo.title: The Lord of the Rings

entries.title: Caradhras Defeats The Company Without A Simple Villain

context.label: Caradhras Defeats The Company Without A Simple Villain

content.fact: Caradhras shows weather, mountain hostility, exposure, and uncertainty defeating the Company.
content.injection: Make Caradhras a genuine environmental crisis, not a minor delay or obvious enemy ambush.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Watcher In The Water Seals The Moria Route

context.label: Watcher at the West-gate before the Moria passage

content.fact: At the West-gate of Moria, the Watcher in the Water attacks and helps cut off easy retreat, committing the Fellowship to the dark passage through Moria.
content.injection: During the West-gate scene, use the Watcher as route pressure: once the gate is reached and attacked, Moria becomes a trap-like commitment rather than a casual travel choice.
constraints: The Watcher should increase urgency and limit retreat.
The danger is local to the Moria entrance, not a general aquatic monster encounter.
antiLore: Do not let the Fellowship simply reconsider and leave safely after this beat.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

content.fact: Lothlorien shelters the grieving Fellowship while testing trust, desire, memory, and burden.
content.injection: Lorien scenes should be healing and unsettling at once: beauty, old power, grief, and inward testing.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

coordinates.label: Book II: The Ring Goes South
Company of the Ring

entries.title: Lothlorien Shelters And Tests The Fellowship

content.fact: Lothlorien gives the Fellowship refuge after Moria, but its Elven power and Galadriel's insight also test motives, grief, and temptation.
content.injection: In Lothlorien scenes, combine sanctuary with unease: the Fellowship is protected and healed, but also spiritually examined and confronted with what they carry.
constraints: Gimli's response to Galadriel can begin shifting his view of Elves.
The refuge should not feel mundane or politically neutral.
antiLore: Do not make Lothlorien a generic safe inn.
Do not omit the sense that Galadriel perceives more than ordinary hosts.
